sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Australian court fines Exxon's local petrol brand $11.3 million for misleading claims
feed.summary: The Federal Court of Australia has fined Mobil Oil Australia A$16 million ($11.3 million) for misleading claims about fuel sold at its petrol stations in Queensland. The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) had taken the local Exxon Mobil unit to court, alleging the company falsely indicated its "Mobil Synergy Fuel" contained certain additives between August 2020 and July 2024. The fuel was found to be the same or substantially similar to unadditised fuel at other non-Mobil retail sites.

feed.title: Peter Thiel's Palantir Extends Partnership With Boeing Rival Airbus
feed.summary: Palantir Technologies Inc. announced the expansion of its multi-year partnership with Airbus SE, focusing on their jointly developed Skywise open data platform. The platform, which integrates in-flight engineering and operational data, is used by over 50,000 users. This news comes amidst skepticism from investor Michael Burry, who predicted a significant stock decline for Palantir.

feed.title: How ConocoPhillips’ (COP) Norway Reinvestment and Capital Returns Have Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips (COP) recently reported lower Q4 2025 earnings year-over-year but affirmed its Q1 2026 dividend and updated production guidance. A significant new development is the company's US$2.10 billion commitment to restart production at three oil fields in Norway's Greater Ekofisk area, which reinforces its strategy of investing in large, capital-intensive oil and gas projects. The success of this and other major projects will be crucial to ConocoPhillips' long-term investment narrative and financial performance.

feed.title: Costco Hit with Class Action Over Alleged Salmonella Risks at Facility
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corp. is facing a class-action lawsuit in federal court over allegations of misleading consumers about the safety of its rotisserie chicken. The lawsuit claims Costco's Nebraska poultry facility repeatedly failed federal Salmonella standards, receiving the lowest food safety rating in approximately 92% of reporting periods since 2019. Plaintiffs contend that Costco aggressively marketed its rotisserie chicken as safe while allegedly omitting persistent contamination issues at its Lincoln Premium Poultry complex.

feed.title: BlackRock Touts 6%–7% Fee Growth, Tokenized iShares and Digital Wallet Push at BofA Conference
feed.summary: BlackRock's CFO Martin Small highlighted the firm's growth drivers at the BofA Financial Services Conference, noting consistent organic base-fee growth of 6-7%. Key priorities include integrating recent acquisitions, a $400 billion fundraising target, and expanding iShares and Aladdin. The company is also making a significant push into digital-wallet distribution with plans for tokenized iShares, engaging the SEC for potential launch within months to a year.

feed.title: 5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From monday.com's Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: monday.com's Q4 2025 results led to a negative market reaction despite strong revenue growth, due to concerns about profitability and future momentum, especially with self-serve customers. This article highlights five key questions analysts posed during the earnings call, touching on decelerating growth drivers, margin compression, AI integration, conservative long-term guidance, and competitive positioning of new products. The company's management addressed these concerns, pointing to increased investments in sales-led growth and AI, and the shift towards upmarket expansion.

feed.title: Exclusive | Paramount grows more confident Warner Bros. Discovery will drop Netflix bid
feed.summary: Paramount Skydance is increasingly confident that Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) will abandon its deal with Netflix, potentially reopening a bidding war for the company. Despite an already "sweetened" offer from Paramount, regulatory hurdles and valuation concerns regarding the Netflix deal are reportedly pushing WBD to reconsider. Investors are growing wary of the Netflix offer's terms and the potential for significant antitrust challenges, even as WBD recently accepted Netflix's bid over Paramount's.

feed.title: Environmental groups oppose Georgia Power expansion powering data centers
feed.summary: Environmental groups are challenging Georgia Power's approved plan to expand its energy grid by 10 gigawatts, intended to meet the rising demand from data centers. Critics, like the Southern Environmental Law Center, argue that the $15.6 billion expansion is excessive and could burden consumers with costs if data centers don't materialize as expected. The Public Service Commission, which approved the expansion, maintains the decision was necessary and has safeguards in place to protect customers, stating that residential and small business customers will not bear higher bills due to data center energy needs.

feed.title: Citigroup maintains CRSP (CRISPR Therapeutics AG) at Buy Feb 2026
feed.summary: Citigroup maintained its "Buy" rating for CRISPR Therapeutics AG (CRSP) on February 16, 2026, and increased its price target from $77 to $80. This action reflects the firm's continued confidence in the company's valuation and pipeline progress. While the increase is modest, it suggests a constructive view for investors, who should consider this alongside other research and company catalysts.

feed.title: Faraday Future Announces Improvement Plan for EAI Robotics
feed.summary: Faraday Future is implementing an improvement plan for its EAI Robotics division and recommending changes for AIxC to enhance its strategic focus and market competitiveness in the EV sector. The company also plans legal action against false information and illegal short selling while emphasizing its ownership of intellectual property related to the Super One project. Despite progress with a Chinese automotive partner, market performance remains weak, impacting investor returns and highlighting execution deficiencies.

feed.title: The Household Brand Behind Walmart's Great Value Bakery Products
feed.summary: Walmart's Great Value bakery items, such as Danishes and cinnamon rolls, are often produced by major food manufacturers, with the J.M. Smucker Company being a key supplier. This connection was revealed through a recall in 2024 for plastic contamination in Great Value products. Other brands like Sara Lee Bakery (now Bimbo Bakeries) and Flowers Foods have also supplied Great Value products, highlighting Walmart's strategy of partnering with established manufacturers for its private-label goods.

feed.title: F5, Inc. Deadline Tomorrow: Rosen Law Firm Urges F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) Stockholders to Contact the Firm for Information About Their Rights
feed.summary: Rosen Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that F5, Inc. misled investors regarding its projected revenue, growth, and risks, resulting in investor damages when the true details were revealed. Stockholders interested in serving as lead plaintiff must file their motion by February 17, 2026.

feed.title: Man Signs Up For Wells Fargo Account. 5 Years Later, They Change Their Rule—And Start Charging Him Monthly Fees
feed.summary: A man is accusing Wells Fargo of suddenly increasing his checking account fees five years after he opened the account, changing the minimum balance required for free checking from $500 to $1,500. While the bank started rolling out this change in late 2025, customers were supposedly informed via bank statements. The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau states that banks must give 30 days' notice for unfavorable term changes, and Wells Fargo has a history of penalties for fraudulent fee practices.

feed.title: This Italian Restaurant Chain Boasts A World-Renowned Chef
feed.summary: Maggiano's Little Italy, an Italian restaurant chain, has appointed world-renowned Chef Anthony Amoroso as its vice president of innovation and growth to elevate its menu of Italian-American classics. He previously worked at two Michelin-starred restaurants and competed on "Iron Chef America." This move is part of Maggiano's turnaround strategy, which has included initiatives like upgrading dishes and streamlining kitchen operations, aiming to revitalize sales and boost guest experience.

feed.title: |【即時新聞】iShares ESG Aware Aggressive Allocation ETF 宣布季度股息發放，股息收益吸引投資人
feed.summary: |iShares ESG Aware Aggressive Allocation ETF (EAOA) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.3459 per share, payable on December 29th, with a record date of December 23rd. This announcement highlights EAOA's stable dividend growth, making it attractive to investors seeking consistent income. The ETF's recent market performance showed a 0.48% increase to $41.7920 and a significant rise in trading volume, indicating growing investor interest.

feed.title: Expeditors International stock: Analyst estimates & ratings
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of analyst estimates and ratings for Expeditors International stock. It is a financial piece focusing on market analysis.

feed.title: UBS Maintains Neutral on BAX Baxter International Inc. Feb 2026
feed.summary: UBS has maintained its Neutral rating on Baxter International Inc. (BAX) and lowered its price target from $21 to $19, following the company's Q4 2025 report and cautious 2026 guidance. This action signals limited near-term upside due to margin pressure and a modest EPS target. Investors are advised to monitor Baxter's execution against its 2026 plan and margin recovery, with Meyka AI also rating BAX with a 'B' grade, acknowledging solid fundamentals against near-term risks.

feed.title: Forex Signals Feb 7: Palo Alto PANW, Coca-Cola, and Medtronic Earnings Preview
feed.summary: This article previews upcoming earnings reports for Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP), and Medtronic (MDT), highlighting investor focus areas for each. It also discusses recent subdued market activity due to holidays and geopolitical tensions, alongside volatility in gold, Bitcoin, and Ethereum prices. Traders are advised to monitor forward guidance for these companies due to elevated valuations.

feed.title: HighPeak Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ:HPK) Short Interest Up 24.8% in January
feed.summary: HighPeak Energy's (NASDAQ:HPK) short interest increased by 24.8% in January, reaching 8,077,153 shares. The days-to-cover ratio stands at 7.5 days, with 28.6% of the company's stock currently sold short. Despite institutional investors like State Street and Charles Schwab increasing their stakes, analyst sentiment remains weak, with a consensus "Reduce" rating and a target price of $11.67.

feed.title: UBS Maintains Neutral on Mohawk Industries, Inc. (MHK) Feb 16, 2026; PT $140
feed.summary: UBS has maintained its Neutral rating on Mohawk Industries, Inc. (MHK) but raised its price target to $140 from $134, following the company's Q4 results and near-term guidance. This signals cautious optimism, as the rating suggests monitoring rather than immediate trading, despite the implied modest upside. The firm cited steady cash flow and margin resilience as reasons for the increased price target, while continued uncertainty in the housing market prevents a more bullish upgrade.

feed.title: Yum! Brands, Inc. (NYSE:YUM) Sees Large Drop in Short Interest
feed.summary: Yum! Brands (NYSE:YUM) experienced a significant 18.8% decrease in short interest in January, with total short interest falling to 7,008,607 shares, representing about 2.5% of outstanding shares. The company recently reported quarterly EPS of $1.73, missing estimates by $0.03, but saw revenue increase by 6.4% to $2.51 billion, exceeding consensus. Yum! Brands also raised its quarterly dividend to $0.75 per share, providing a 1.9% yield.

feed.title: Decoding early synaptic failure in Alzheimer’s disease through multiomic analysis
feed.summary: This article announces a webinar titled "Decoding early synaptic failure through multiomics," focusing on Alzheimer's disease. The webinar will delve into a multiomic reanalysis of synaptosome data, integrating mRNA and miRNA data to reveal regulatory signals underlying synaptic dysfunction. It aims to demonstrate practical multiomic workflows using Illumina Connected Multiomics software for Alzheimer's disease research.

feed.title: Linde stock heads into Presidents Day break up 1.7% — what to watch for LIN next
feed.summary: Linde (LIN) stock rose 1.7% to $481 a share before the Presidents Day holiday. Investors are focused on the company's 2026 profit targets, capital spending plans, and upcoming remarks from its CFO at two investor conferences this week. The outlook for pricing power amid varying industrial demand and the impact of rising capital spending are key concerns for the stock.

feed.title: Watch Rollins CFO’s Barclays conference talk live Feb. 18
feed.summary: Rollins, Inc. (NYSE: ROL) announced that its Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, Kenneth Krause, will present at the Barclays 43rd Annual Industrial Select Conference on February 18, 2026. The presentation will be webcast live from 1:50 p.m. to 2:20 p.m. ET and a replay will be available for 180 days on the company's investor page. Rollins is a global consumer and commercial services company specializing in pest control.

feed.title: Apple to stream video podcasts on its platforms
feed.summary: Apple has announced that video podcasting will now be available on its platforms, entering a space already occupied by YouTube, Netflix, and Spotify. This move positions Apple to compete directly with these platforms by offering users the ability to both listen to and watch podcasts, with the flexibility to switch between formats. The announcement follows other recent podcast-related deals in the industry, indicating a growing trend in video podcast content.

feed.title: Teledyne FLIR OEM Expands Neutrino ISR Series with Longest-Range Model
feed.summary: Teledyne FLIR OEM has expanded its Neutrino ISR Series with the launch of the Neutrino SX8 ISR 50-1000, which is their longest-range model to date. This high-performance thermal imaging solution features a 20x continuous zoom and is designed for demanding long-range intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) applications such as border security and counter-UAS missions. It offers significant detection, recognition, and identification ranges and incorporates advanced features like AI readiness and seamless integration capabilities.

feed.title: Rollins to Present at Upcoming Investor Conference
feed.summary: Rollins, Inc. announced that its Executive Vice President and CFO, Kenneth Krause, will present at the Barclays 43rd Annual Industrial Select Conference. The presentation is scheduled for Wednesday, February 18, from 1:50 p.m. to 2:20 p.m. E.T. and will be webcast live on the company's investor relations website.

feed.title: Public Storage to relocate to North Texas
feed.summary: Public Storage, a company that has been based in Southern California for over 50 years, is reportedly relocating its operations to North Texas. The article, while brief, highlights this significant move for the company.

feed.title: Xcel Energy may cut power to 15,000 in Texas Panhandle amid wildfire risk Tuesday
feed.summary: Xcel Energy plans to implement a "Public Safety Power Shut Off" (PSPS) starting Tuesday morning, potentially affecting 15,000 people across 16 counties in the Texas Panhandle. This measure is a proactive effort to reduce wildfire risk due to high winds and dry conditions. Power restoration after such an event could take several hours to multiple days as crews need to check for damage before re-energizing lines.

feed.title: Does Illinois Tool Works’ (ITW) Rising Payout and Buybacks Redefine Its Capital Allocation Priorities?
feed.summary: Illinois Tool Works (ITW) reported strong Q4 2025 results with increased sales and net income, alongside significant share repurchases and a declared dividend, reinforcing its commitment to returning capital to shareholders. The company's investment narrative focuses on steady cash generation, disciplined capital returns, and modest growth, which is supported by its 2026 revenue and EPS guidance. While the company's dividend and buyback strategies are reassuring, investors should be mindful of potential segment weaknesses that could challenge profit targets.

feed.title: Halozyme Therapeutics Q4 Earnings Preview
feed.summary: Halozyme Therapeutics (HALO) is poised to release its Q4 earnings on February 17th, with analysts anticipating a robust 74.6% year-over-year EPS growth to $2.20 and a 49.7% rise in revenue to $446.13 million. The company has a strong record of beating EPS and revenue estimates, and recent upward revisions reflect analysts' optimistic outlook. Despite a recent slight price target downgrade by Morgan Stanley, TD Cowen upgraded its target, signaling continued market confidence.

feed.title: Global MA Q4 2025 Market Update
feed.summary: Global M&A activity in Q4 2025 was characterized by strategic intent, with strategic buyers driving most deals and intra-industry transactions being more prevalent. While some sectors showed stability, overall activity was measured, with a notable increase in AI-related M&A. This trend of strategic repositioning and thematic acquisitions is expected to continue into 2026.

feed.title: Short Interest in Goldman Sachs BDC, Inc. (NYSE:GSBD) Increases By 25.5%
feed.summary: Short interest in Goldman Sachs BDC, Inc. (NYSE:GSBD) increased by 25.5% in January, reaching 2,633,872 shares, which represents about 2.3% of shares outstanding. The short-interest ratio is currently 1.9 days based on average trading volume. Shares of GSBD traded down 2.0% to $9.23, and analysts maintain an average "Reduce" rating with a $9.75 price target.

feed.title: BGSF, Inc. Announces Timing of Fiscal 2025 Third Quarter Results and Earnings Conference Call
feed.summary: BGSF, Inc. (NYSE:BGSF) is set to release its fiscal 2025 third quarter results on Wednesday, November 5, 2025, after market close. Following the release, management will host an earnings conference call and webcast on Thursday, November 6, 2025, at 9:00 am ET. Call and replay details have been provided for interested participants and investors.

feed.title: How Camden’s Dividend Hike, Impairment Charge, And New Buyback Plan At Camden Property Trust (CPT) Has Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Camden Property Trust reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings, an impairment charge, and issued 2026 EPS guidance. The company also increased its quarterly dividend to US$1.06 per share and authorized a new US$600 million share repurchase program, signaling a focus on returning capital to shareholders. While these moves reinforce management's commitment to shareholders, investors should remain aware of potential risks from elevated new apartment supply in key Sun Belt markets.

feed.title: Hardware is King: Applied Materials Defies AI Software Slump with Massive Breakout
feed.summary: Applied Materials (NASDAQ: AMAT) experienced a significant breakout, with its shares surging over 8.1% to a record $354.91, amidst a broader "SaaSpocalypse" affecting AI software companies. This surge was driven by strong Q1 fiscal earnings, which beat analyst expectations, and positive forward-looking guidance, including a projected 20% growth for the semiconductor equipment business in 2026. The company also resolved a long-standing regulatory risk with a $252.5 million settlement, marking a shift in market sentiment towards hardware infrastructure providers over AI software services due to increasing ROI skepticism and automation concerns.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries and Renesas Expand Strategic Collaboration to Enhance Semiconductor Manufacturing and Supply Chain Resilience
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries and Renesas Electronics have announced an expanded multi-billion-dollar strategic partnership to strengthen semiconductor supply chains for automotive and IoT applications. This collaboration will give Renesas access to GlobalFoundries' advanced technology platforms, with manufacturing planned across the U.S., Germany, Singapore, and potential integration into Renesas' Japanese facilities. The initiative aims to support the growing demand for semiconductors in advanced automotive technologies and enhance domestic production for economic and national security.

feed.title: Every New Car Offering 0% Financing in February 2026
feed.summary: This article lists new cars offering 0% financing in February 2026, highlighting the Kia EV9, Ram 1500, and Ineos Grenadier. It explains the benefits of 0% APR, stating that it can save buyers thousands of dollars, though not everyone with excellent credit will qualify. The article also provides a comprehensive table of models, brands, and terms for 0% financing offers, most of which expire on March 2, 2026.

feed.title: CSX's Advanced Locomotive Technology Reduces Fuel Consumption and Emissions
feed.summary: CSX has significantly reduced fuel consumption and emissions through enhancements to its locomotive technology, primarily with the Automatic Engine Stop-Start (AESS) system. An upgrade in 2024, the AESS Assist system, further improved shutdown performance and battery support, leading to increased fuel efficiency and extended battery life. Collaborating with Wabtec, CSX also implemented software upgrades to prolong engine shutdown times, reinforcing its commitment to lower operational costs and environmental sustainability.

feed.title: Multibillion-dollar chip deal aims to steady supply for smarter cars, factories
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries (GFS) and Renesas have announced an expanded multibillion-dollar manufacturing partnership to improve semiconductor supply resilience and accelerate U.S. chip manufacturing. The collaboration will broaden Renesas's access to GF's differentiated technologies, including FDX, BCD, and CMOS with non-volatile memory, to support various applications in automotive and industrial sectors. Manufacturing is set to begin in mid-2026 across GF's global footprint, with considerations for porting select GF processes into Renesas's Japan fabs.

feed.title: When the Price of (FRX) Talks, People Listen (FRX:CA)
feed.summary: This article provides AI-generated trading signals and plans for Fennec Pharmaceuticals Inc. (FRX:CA). It outlines specific buy and short targets with stop losses, along with a rating of "Weak Neutral Strong" for different investment terms. The piece also lists numerous past articles related to FRX, highlighting ongoing analysis and trading advice.

feed.title: Buy This, Not That: 2026 Subaru Outback or 2026 Toyota 4Runner?
feed.summary: This article compares the 2026 Subaru Outback and 2026 Toyota 4Runner, two off-road-oriented SUVs catering to outdoor enthusiasts, to help potential buyers decide. It highlights their differences in design, interior space, powertrains, and pricing, noting the Outback's car-based practicality versus the 4Runner's truck-based ruggedness. The decision largely depends on whether the buyer prioritizes everyday comfort and affordability or superior off-road capability and towing.

feed.title: Maintenance will cause railroad crossing closures in Bryan
feed.summary: The City of Bryan announced that Union Pacific Railroad will be conducting maintenance projects, leading to several railroad crossing closures over the next two weeks. Work hours will be from 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. daily, starting February 17th. Message boards will provide advance notice and detour information, though dates are subject to change.

feed.title: China's BYD Just Beat Ford in Global Sales for the First Time Ever
feed.summary: In 2025, China's BYD Company surpassed Ford Motor Company in global vehicle sales for the first time, delivering approximately 4.6 million vehicles compared to Ford's 4.4 million. This achievement highlights BYD's focus on new energy vehicles (NEVs) and its significant growth in both domestic and international markets, now ranking as the sixth-largest global automaker. The shift underscores a broader transformation in the automotive industry as electric and plug-in hybrid vehicles gain market dominance.

feed.title: Energy Brief: Earnings, Rates, Solar, Global Energy Moves
feed.summary: This energy brief covers upcoming earnings reports from major energy companies, challenges to utility rate increases, and efforts to plug abandoned oil wells. It also highlights NextEra Energy's dividend increase and global energy developments, including Iran's nuclear agreement talks, agrivoltaics research, India's solar capacity surge, and the Philippines' green energy auction plans.

feed.title: First Trust Small Cap Value AlphaDEX Fund (NASDAQ:FYT) Short Interest Update
feed.summary: Short interest in First Trust Small Cap Value AlphaDEX Fund (NASDAQ:FYT) significantly decreased by 17.8% to 9,482 shares as of January 30th. This translates to 0.4% of shares outstanding and a days-to-cover ratio of 0.9. Several institutional investors increased their stakes, including Jones Financial Companies and Cetera Investment Advisers, while the fund recently raised its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: US Blackstone invests $600M in India’s AI company Neysa
feed.summary: US private equity firm Blackstone has led a primary equity investment of up to $600 million in India-based AI infrastructure startup Neysa. This investment will grant Blackstone a majority stake in Neysa, with co-investors also participating. The move aims to significantly boost India's domestic computing capacity, as the country seeks to develop its homegrown AI capabilities amidst rapidly increasing demand for local compute infrastructure.

feed.title: Motorcar Parts of America: A Strategic Pivot Toward Recovery ()
feed.summary: Motorcar Parts of America is undergoing a strategic operational transition following a challenging third quarter impacted by reduced orders from a major client. The company is evaluating strategic alternatives for its EV technology division, focusing on operational efficiencies in brake components, and expanding into heavy-duty parts and the Mexican market. Upcoming Q4 and full fiscal year 2026 results are expected to provide insights into the recovery of customer orders and the effectiveness of these strategic initiatives.

feed.title: A Look At New Oriental Education & Technology Group (EDU) Valuation After Recent Mixed Share Price Performance
feed.summary: New Oriental Education & Technology Group (EDU) has shown mixed share price performance recently, with a 6% increase over the last month but a slight decline in shorter periods. Despite this, the company's valuation narrative suggests it is about 9% undervalued, with a fair value of US$64.49 against a current share price of US$58.70. Analysts believe strong growth in non-academic tutoring and AI-powered learning products will drive long-term revenue growth and improve margins, although risks like competition and potential setbacks in newer ventures exist.

feed.title: CoreWeave, Inc. (CRWV) Investors: March 13, 2026 Filing Deadline in Securities Class Action - Contact Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP – Company Announcement - FT.com
feed.summary: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP has filed a securities fraud class action lawsuit against CoreWeave, Inc. (NASDAQ: CRWV) on behalf of investors who purchased securities between March 28, 2025, and December 15, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that CoreWeave made false or misleading statements regarding its ability to meet customer demand and the risks associated with relying on a single third-party data center supplier. The deadline for investors to seek appointment as lead plaintiff is March 13, 2026.
